headline: Your founder waterfall journey.
subheadline: A scrollable GTMx strategy artifact for routing funded tech founders into the right Mentor Capital offer.
productSummary: SageCreek Partners is an operator-led Mentor Capital advisory: seasoned executives who sit beside founders to raise capital, build technology, grow sales, and drive lucrative exits.
coreInsight: A tech company’s funding stage and maturity tell us exactly which SageCreek capability the founder needs most right now: exit facilitation, GTM scale, capital raising, or operator mentorship.

sampleValues.{{ai_capital_idea}}: tighten the enterprise security narrative before the next raise
sampleValues.{{ai_gtm_idea}}: turn founder-led proof into one repeatable mid-market motion

sections.label: Signal
sections.title: The signal ladder is the company lifecycle
sections.takeaway: Funding stage, company maturity, and active GTM hiring reveal which SageCreek capability a founder needs now.

sections.label: Waterfall
sections.title: One cascade routes the whole founder TAM
sections.takeaway: Every company is tested top-to-bottom by intent: exit-ready, sales scaling, capital raising, then the mentor catch-all.

sections.label: Tiers
sections.title: Each maturity stage gets a different offer
sections.takeaway: The highest-value founders get exit readiness. Earlier founders get GTM, capital, or operator mentorship depending on the trigger.

sections.label: Campaigns
sections.title: Nineteen angles layered on the waterfall
sections.takeaway: The core waterfall carries the volume. Side campaigns stay time-boxed for fresh raises, new sales leaders, and portfolio buyers.

sections.label: Email Copy
sections.title: Copy that sells operator proof, not consulting fluff
sections.takeaway: Each sequence uses a specific signal, pokes the founder risk, then offers a low-friction teardown or gut-check.

sections.label: Launch
sections.title: Start where the proof is hardest to ignore
sections.takeaway: Launch exit-readiness first, then capital raising, sales scaling, and finally the mentor catch-all.

signalReasons: Crunchbase and PitchBook reveal funding stage, total raised, company age, last funding date, and investor type in one pull.
LinkedIn Jobs adds the Tier 2 buying signal: active SDR, AE, CRO, or VP Sales hiring in the last 90 days.
The signal maps directly to real SageCreek capabilities: exits, revenue build, capital raising, and operator mentorship.
A catch-all Founder Gut-Check keeps 100% TAM coverage for every remaining founder or CEO.

sources.name: Crunchbase / PitchBook
sources.delivers: Funding stage, last funding date, total raised, founded year, investor type, and TAM

sources.name: LinkedIn Sales Navigator + Jobs
sources.delivers: Founder/CEO identification and current sales/GTM hiring signal

sources.name: YC Work / Wellfound
sources.delivers: Startup hiring backfill for active GTM and leadership roles

sources.name: Apollo
sources.delivers: Founder/CEO email, title, headcount tier, and HQ region

sources.name: Website scrape
sources.delivers: Software/tech sanity check and AI-generated company focus

sources.name: Portfolio / investor research
sources.delivers: Side-campaign routes for VC, PE, and portfolio value-creation buyers

routingRules.if: company_age >= 7 AND (stage in {C+/late, PE-backed} OR total_raised >= $20M) AND headcount in {51-200, 200+}
stage in {A, B} AND hiring_sales_roles_90d = true
stage in {bootstrapped, pre-seed, seed} AND (last_funding_date > 18 months ago OR stage = bootstrapped)
every remaining tech founder or CEO in TAM

tiers.name: Tier 1
tiers.label: Exit-ready
tiers.trigger: Founded 7+ years, Series C+/late/PE-backed or $20M+ raised, 51+ headcount
tiers.pain: The founder is approaching the highest-stakes decision of their life and may be outmatched by buyers who have done this dozens of times.
tiers.valueProp: SageCreek has led nine companies past $1B and sat beside founders through multi-billion-dollar exits.
tiers.replyDensity: Highest deal value, judge on pipeline quality

tiers.name: Tier 2
tiers.label: Sales scaling
tiers.trigger: Series A/B and hiring SDR, AE, CRO, or VP Sales roles in the last 90 days
tiers.pain: The board wants revenue post-raise, but the founder may be over-hiring before the sales motion exists.
tiers.valueProp: Operators who built revenue orgs at scale install the motion before the round gets torched on headcount.
tiers.replyDensity: Strong practical pain after recent raise

tiers.name: Tier 3
tiers.label: Capital raising
tiers.trigger: Bootstrapped, or seed/pre-seed with no follow-on in 18+ months
tiers.pain: Runway pressure, stalled between rounds, weak investor story, or thin next-check network.
tiers.valueProp: The Capital in Mentor Capital is literal: SageCreek invests, opens the network, and gets founders fundable.

tiers.name: Catch-all
tiers.label: Mentor
tiers.trigger: Every remaining tech founder or CEO in the TAM
tiers.pain: Every founder eventually hits a wall they have never seen before.
tiers.valueProp: 100+ companies from startup to exit. Less attitude, more altitude.

campaigns.name: Seen It Before Mentor
campaigns.filter: Every remaining tech founder or CEO
campaigns.strategy: Founder-stage context and soft office-hours CTA
campaigns.value: Borrow operators who have walked the road
campaigns.overview: The broad mentor angle for founders hitting unfamiliar walls.

campaigns.name: Creative Ideas / 3 Moves
campaigns.filter: Catch-all founders with usable website signal
campaigns.strategy: Generate 3 operator moves from site plus stage
campaigns.overview: A value-first variant that proves SageCreek thinks like operators.

campaigns.name: Fundability Teardown
campaigns.value: Get fundable and open the network
campaigns.overview: Shows why investors may be passing and one concrete fix.

campaigns.name: GTM / Sales-Motion Teardown
campaigns.strategy: Open sales role plus post-raise pressure
campaigns.value: Hit the number without torching the round
campaigns.overview: Frames new sales hiring as risky before the motion exists.

campaigns.name: Exit-Readiness Scorecard
campaigns.strategy: Age, cap table, and exit timing
campaigns.overview: The highest-value offer for founders approaching a once-in-a-lifetime exit.

campaigns.name: New Founder-Hire / New CEO
campaigns.filter: New founder CEO or outside CEO
campaigns.overview: A time-boxed side play for new leaders.

campaigns.name: Altiris Lookalike
campaigns.overview: Uses Greg Butterfield and Altiris as the proof anchor.

campaigns.name: Fresh-Raise, First 90 Days
campaigns.overview: Triggered burst for founders whose board pressure just increased.

campaigns.name: Board-Pressure / 2nd-Round Stall
campaigns.overview: Sharper capital-raising pain for founders between rounds.

campaigns.name: VP-Sales Hire Without a Playbook
campaigns.filter: New VP Sales or CRO hiring signal
campaigns.strategy: Handoff risk before sales leader starts
campaigns.value: Set the new sales leader up to win

campaigns.name: PE-Backed Value Creation
campaigns.value: Hit the plan the sponsor is counting on
campaigns.overview: A sponsor-pressure spin on the exit-readiness tier.

campaigns.name: LatAm / EMEA Founder Bridge
campaigns.filter: EMEA or LatAm tech company expanding US
campaigns.overview: Uses SageCreek's US, EMEA, and LatAm footprint.

campaigns.name: Technical Founder, No Commercial Co-Founder
campaigns.filter: Technical-led company with weak commercial leadership
campaigns.overview: Capital and GTM help for founders missing a commercial counterpart.

campaigns.name: Podcast / Stage Founder
campaigns.filter: Founder with public quote, podcast, or talk
campaigns.overview: Manual-research credibility for warmer founder conversations.

campaigns.name: Repeat / Second-Time Founder
campaigns.overview: A more respectful angle for founders who have done it before.

campaigns.name: Investor / Portfolio Companion
campaigns.overview: Different buyer, so it stays outside the founder waterfall.

campaigns.name: Rumored-Exit / Banker-Hired
campaigns.filter: Banker, M&A, or exit rumor signal
campaigns.value: Do not get outmatched by buyers
campaigns.overview: Urgent Tier 1 variant when exit motion is visible.

campaigns.name: Down-Round / Bridge Risk
campaigns.overview: Capital-raising help for founders facing a tough market.

campaigns.name: Golden ICP
campaigns.overview: The most manual, most valuable Tier 1 motion.

messages.subject: nine to a billion
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company_name}} is {{company_age}} years in with {{investor_name}} on the cap table. Usually the point where the exit conversation stops being hypothetical.

But when it comes, will you be across the table from buyers who have done this 50 times while you have done it once?

Our team has led nine companies past $1B and sat beside founders through multi-billion-dollar exits. In the room, not on a slide.

Reply "scorecard" and I'll send our exit-readiness checklist, 8 things that add a turn to your multiple. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the mistake I watch good founders make: they wait until a banker knocks to get ready.

By then the leverage is gone. Bankers run the process for the buyer's benefit as much as yours.

We sit on your side of the table before that, so the terms, the story, and the numbers are built to hold up under diligence.

Should I send the scorecard so you can see where {{company_name}} stands today?

messages.subject: exit readiness
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick one on {{company_name}}. Most founders only sell a company once, so "exit-ready" is a muscle they never got to build.

We have built it nine times.

No need for a call. Just reply "yes" and I'll send the exit-readiness scorecard. Should I send it to you, or is {{cfo_or_board_contact}} the better person?

messages.subject: {{open_role}} hire
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company_name}} is hiring {{open_roles_count}} sales roles right now, including {{open_role}}.

But are you building the motion by feel and hoping the new hires reverse-engineer it once they land?

Most post-Series-A teams burn a chunk of the round hiring reps before the playbook exists. We install the motion first so the headcount funds growth, not guesswork.

Our operators have scaled revenue orgs inside companies we took past $1B.

Reply "teardown" and I'll send a breakdown of your current GTM setup. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, the math that gets founders in trouble: three AEs at full comp is roughly {{est_annual_cost}} a year before a single deal closes.

If the motion is not dialed, that is expensive tuition.

We sequence it the other way. Define the motion, prove it, then hire into a machine that already works.

Want the GTM teardown so you can see where the leaks are before the next hire starts?

messages.subject: {{company_name}} GTM
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick one. Founder-led sales rarely transfers cleanly.

Most first sales hires miss their first two quarters, not because they are bad, but because there was no motion to step into.

We have built that handoff nine times.

No call needed. Reply "yes" and I'll send the GTM teardown. You, or is {{sales_leader_contact}} the right person for this?

messages.subject: the next round
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, saw {{company_name}} raised {{last_round}} back in {{last_funding_date}} and has been heads-down since.

But would it be fair to assume the next round is the thing quietly keeping you up at night?

The "Capital" in Mentor Capital is literal. We invest, and we open our network to get founders fundable, then in the room. We have helped 100-plus companies get from stuck to funded to exited.

Reply "teardown" and I'll send a read on why investors might be passing on you. No call needed.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, from experience, investors rarely pass on the product. They pass on the story and the numbers around it.

We rebuild both before you walk into the room, then warm-intro you to people who actually write checks at your stage.

That is the difference between a cold raise and a real process.

Want the fundability teardown so you can see what a partner would flag first?

messages.subject: why they pass
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick one on {{company_name}}. Most founders try to raise from the outside in, pitching strangers cold.

We have been on the other side of the table 100-plus times and know what makes a partner lean in.

No call needed. Reply "yes" and I'll send the fundability teardown. Should I send it to you or {{cofounder_contact}}?

messages.subject: less attitude, more altitude
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, been reading about what you are building at {{company_name}} around {{ai_company_focus}}.

But would it be fair to say you are hitting problems no one on your cap table has actually solved before?

That is the whole reason SageCreek exists. Operators who have been founder, CEO, and board, across 100-plus companies from startup to exit.

Less attitude, more altitude.

No pitch. Reply "yes" and I'll set up a 20-minute founder gut-check with one of our operators. Bring the thing you are stuck on.

messages.subject: threaded follow-up
seen it before
messages.body:
{{first_name}}, quick one. The expensive part of building a company is learning every lesson the hard way, on your own clock.

Our whole model is letting founders borrow ours instead.

No call pitch, just a real conversation. Reply "yes" and I'll book a 20-minute founder gut-check. You, or is {{cofounder_contact}} the one wrestling with this?

launchPlan: Launch Tier 1 Exit-Readiness first. It is the smallest list, sharpest pain, and SageCreek's billion-dollar-exit proof is unmatched here.
Launch Tier 3 Capital Raising second. It is the largest sharp-pain pool with a resonant offer.
Launch Tier 2 Sales Scaling once the LinkedIn hiring-signal pull is wired.
Turn on the catch-all Mentor campaign as the always-on sweep for everything unmatched.
Add investor / portfolio side campaigns only after the founder waterfall is stable.

thresholds.action: Cut and rework. For Tier 1, also weight pipeline quality.
